fields.description:
## Context
Stamp denormalized author identity (name + email) plus an OPTIONAL `author_id`
(referencing a ddbe `identity`) onto EVERY mutating event's envelope, without
breaking replay of pre-change events. Substrate for authorship attestation (bde5)
and enforcement (3183). Hard-depends on ddbe (identity entity + resolver).

## Why
Authorship certification needs each event to record who wrote it in a replication-
and compaction-durable form (denormalized), with an optional link to the rich
`identity` entity when one resolves (identity is opt-in).

## What
### 1. Shared attribution helper (`src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py`)
- Extract `_git_config(key, fallback="") -> str` from the existing `author()`;
  reimplement `author()` on it; add `author_email()` = `_git_config("user.email","")`.
  Strip; return fallback on unset / OSError / non-zero exit / timeout; NEVER raise.
- Add `attribution_fields(repo_root=None) -> dict` returning
  `{"author_email": author_email()}` plus `{"author_id": <id>}` ONLY when
  `identity.resolve_current_identity(repo_root=repo_root)` is non-None (ddbe ships
  this keyword-only `(*, repo_root=None)` signature; 4e9d depends on ddbe). Omit
  `author_id` when None.
- CACHE: a module-level dict `_ATTRIBUTION_CACHE: dict[str, dict]` keyed by the
  CANONICAL repo_root string `os.path.realpath(str(repo_root or config.repo_root()))`
  — so `None` (the append_event default) and an explicit path to the SAME physical
  repo resolve to the SAME key (AC5's 'at most once' cannot be split). NOT `lru_cache(maxsize=1)` — a single slot poisons
  multi-repo tests). First call per repo_root does exactly ONE git-email lookup and
  ONE `resolve_current_identity` scan; subsequent calls for that repo_root return the
  cached dict. (Process-scoped; never persisted. A test helper may clear it.)

### 2. Stamp at ALL FOUR envelope-composing sites (verified complete list)
Every site that COMPOSES an event envelope stamps `author` (name) next to `env_id`.
Add an optional `attribution: dict | None = None` parameter to each composer and do
`if attribution: event.update(attribution)` immediately after the `author` key, so
`author_email`/`author_id` land in the envelope. Callers pass
`attribution_fields(repo_root)` computed WHERE they already compute/hold `author`
and have `repo_root`/tracker in scope. The four sites:
- `_seam.py::append_event` (lines ~219-220) — the majority path (create/edit/link/
  comment/tag/sign/...). Compute `attribution_fields(repo_root)` inline (it already
  receives `repo_root`); include it BEFORE the batch-sink branch so buffered events
  are byte-identical.
- `_commands/txn.py` — `transition_core` (STATUS ~280) and `claim_core` (STATUS ~449,
  EDIT ~468). Add an `attribution: dict | None = None` kwarg to BOTH cores (mirroring
  how `author`/`env_id` are already threaded); the callers `transition_close.py` and
  `claim.py` (which hold `repo_root`) compute it and pass it. Do NOT add a bare
  `repo_root` to the core signatures — thread the ready-made `attribution` dict.
- `_commands/delete.py` — `_write_unlink` (~116) and `_write_event` (~206). Add an
  `attribution: dict | None = None` param to both AND to the intermediate
  `scan_and_write_unlinks` (which sits between `delete_cli` and `_write_unlink` and
  already threads `author`) so the dict reaches `_write_unlink`. The top caller
  (`delete_cli`, which holds repo_root) computes `attribution_fields(...)` once and
  threads it down alongside `author`.
- `_commands/compact.py` — SNAPSHOT composition in `_compact_locked` (~198) and
  `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` (~364), which use `_git_author()`. Compute
  `attribution_fields(repo_root)` alongside and merge it, so SNAPSHOTs carry
  attribution too (otherwise a compacted ticket's fold loses it).

EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE (5th composer, deliberately excluded): the inbound Jira
reconciler composes its OWN envelopes via `_write_event_file()` /`_event_meta()` in
`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py` (~214, called by
`apply_inbound.py`/`apply_inbound_records.py`/`applier.py`). Inbound events attribute
to the EXTERNAL Jira author, NOT the local git user/identity — applying local
`attribution_fields` there would be semantically WRONG. External-author attribution
is the job of the provider-mapping stories (264f outbound, 2f13 inbound ghost). 4e9d
covers the four LOCAL-write composers only.

### 3. Replay back-compat (reducer surfaces PRESENT-ONLY, matching where `author` lives)
Surface present-only (mirrors `source_*`) at the SAME structural location each
processor already puts `author` — verified sites:
- `process_create` (_processors.py:59) → TOP-LEVEL `state["author_email"]`/
  `state["author_id"]` present-only (the ticket-author attribution bde5 reads).
- `process_comment` (:248) → on the per-comment `_entry` dict.
- `process_revert` (:374) → on the per-revert record.
- `process_signature` (:494) → on the signature record.
(`process_edit`/`process_status` do NOT stamp author; NOT touched.) A pre-change
event (no author_email/author_id) reduces BYTE-IDENTICAL — no new keys anywhere.
SNAPSHOT restores compiled_state verbatim (additive; older reducers ignore unknown
keys) — no special handling.

## Scope
In: helpers + repo-keyed cache; stamping at the 4 composer sites via the
`attribution` param; reducer present-only surfacing at the 4 processors; back-compat
+ every-path + cache tests. Out: signing/verifying (bde5), key lifecycle (e165),
enforcement (3183). `author_id` is populated, NOT verified/required here.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EVERY LOCAL mutating path stamps `author_email` into the raw stored event
  (inbound Jira-reconciler events are excluded by design — see scope note; their
  author is the external Jira user, handled by 264f/2f13): a test
  exercises `create` (append_event), `comment` (append_event), a `claim`/`transition`
  (txn.py), a `delete`/archive (delete.py), and a `compact` SNAPSHOT exercising BOTH
  compact.py paths (a threshold `_compact_locked` compaction AND a
  `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` rebuild), asserting each resulting event JSON
  contains `author_email` — proving all four
  composers stamp it, not just append_event.
- [ ] With a current identity set (`rebar identity use <id>`): a CREATE carries
  `author_id == <id>` in the raw event AND top-level reduced state surfaces
  `author_id`/`author_email`. With NO identity resolved: `author_id` is ABSENT from
  both the raw event and reduced state (test both branches).
- [ ] Back-compat: a hand-authored event stream WITHOUT author_email/author_id reduces
  without error to compiled state containing NEITHER key anywhere (top-level or
  sub-records) — byte-identical to pre-change — asserted by diffing the reduced dict.
- [ ] `author_email()` returns `""` (never raises) when `git config user.email` is
  unset or the git call errors — tested by forcing the subprocess to fail.
- [ ] Caching: across N>=3 sequential writes in one process for one repo_root, BOTH
  the git-email lookup AND `resolve_current_identity` run at most once each (NOT
  vacuous: N must be >=3 so 'at most once' is a real reduction from N) — asserted with
  TWO independent call-counters.

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py` (_git_config + author_email + attribution_fields + repo-keyed cache + append_event stamping)
- `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py` (attribution kwarg on transition_core + claim_core)
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` (caller: compute attribution_fields(repo_root), pass to transition_core)
- `src/rebar/_commands/claim.py` (caller: compute attribution_fields(repo_root), pass to claim_core)
- `src/rebar/_commands/delete.py` (attribution param on _write_unlink + _write_event + scan_and_write_unlinks passthrough)
- `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py` (stamp SNAPSHOT envelopes in _compact_locked + rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (present-only surfacing: top-level at process_create; per-entry at comment/revert/signature)
